title: Find Calm with 7 Proven Herbal Essential Oils for Upset Stomach

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for upset stomach because it helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the stomach, which can ease nausea, vomiting, and pain caused by digestive issues.
Shogaol is good for upset stomach because it helps to reduce inflammation and spasms in the digestive tract, allowing the stomach to calm down and recover from irritation.
Zingerone is good for upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms of nausea, bloating, and discomfort.

Zingiberene is good for upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the digestive tract, making it easier to digest food and reducing nausea and discomfort.
Curcumenol is good for an upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive tract.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger essential oil, is a natural remedy for an upset stomach. The oil is extracted from the root of the ginger plant, which has been used for centuries to ease digestive issues. When taken internally or applied topically, ginger oil helps to reduce nausea and vomiting caused by motion sickness, food poisoning, or other stomach problems. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort. Furthermore, ginger oil has antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, easing cramps and spasms. This makes it a popular remedy for stomach issues, such as indigestion, bloating, and diarrhea.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Carum carvi herbal essential oil, which helps with an upset stomach, start by gathering caraway seeds, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a clean glass jar. Begin by drying the caraway seeds in a low-temperature oven or by spreading them out in the sun for a few hours to remove any moisture. Once the seeds are dry, place them in the glass jar and cover them with the carrier oil, making sure the seeds are completely submerged. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for about two weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the oil extract from the seeds. After two weeks, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the seeds. Your homemade caraway essential oil is now ready to use, and you can apply a few drops to your abdomen to help soothe an upset stomach.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for upset stomach

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Menthol is good for an upset stomach because it helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, allowing food to move through the digestive system more easily.
Menthone is good for upset stomach because it helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce inflammation, making it easier to digest food and feel better.

Rosmarinic acid is good for upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract, making it easier to digest food and alleviating nausea and discomfort.
Linalool is good for upset stomach because it helps to reduce inflammation and ease digestive issues by having a calming effect on the stomach muscles.
Limonene is good for upset stomach because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help calm irritation in the stomach and reduce nausea and cramps.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint essential oil, is good for upset stomachs because it helps to calm and soothe the digestive system. When we eat something that doesn't agree with us, it can cause our stomach to feel upset and queasy. Peppermint oil works by relaxing the muscles in the stomach and intestines, which can help to ease cramps and spasms that can make us feel worse. It also has a natural anti-inflammatory effect, which can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive tract. Additionally, peppermint oil has a calming effect on the mind and body, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that can contribute to an upset stomach. When inhaled, peppermint oil can also help to ease nausea and vomiting by stimulating the brain's nausea center to slow down its signals.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Carum carvi herbal essential oils that help with an upset stomach, you'll need fresh or dried caraway seeds. First, harvest or purchase high-quality caraway seeds and dry them completely. Then, place about 1 cup of the dried seeds in a clean glass jar and cover them with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. The general ratio is 1 part seeds to 3 parts oil. Next, store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. This process allows the seeds to infuse their medicinal properties into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container. Discard the seeds and store the infused oil in a dark glass bottle. Keep in mind that the resulting oil may not be as potent as commercial essential oils, but it's a natural and gentle way to use caraway seeds for digestive issues.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for upset stomach

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anethole is good for upset stomach because it helps to relax the muscles in the digestive system and reduce inflammation, which can ease nausea and discomfort.
Polyphenols are good for an upset stomach because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive system and reduce irritation in the stomach lining.
Foeniculin (Caraway seeds) is good for upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing irritation and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oil, also known as anise or fennel oil, is a natural remedy for upset stomach. The oil is extracted from the seeds of the fennel plant, which has been used for centuries to ease digestive issues. When ingested, fennel oil helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause discomfort. Fennel oil also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe an irritated stomach lining and reduce inflammation. Additionally, fennel oil has natural carminative properties, which means it helps to release trapped gas in the digestive system, providing quick relief from bloating and discomfort. By reducing inflammation, relaxing stomach muscles, and releasing trapped gas, fennel oil can provide fast and effective relief from upset stomach and digestive issues.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Carum carvi, also known as Caraway essential oil, which can help with an upset stomach, you will need caraway seeds. First, you will need to dry and harvest the seeds from the caraway plant, which is a flowering plant that belongs to the parsley family. Next, you will need to crush the seeds to release their oils. You can use a mortar and pestle for this. Once the seeds are crushed, you will need to extract the oil from the seeds using a solvent such as jojoba oil or grapeseed oil. This is done by mixing the crushed seeds with the solvent in a clean glass container and letting it sit for a few hours or overnight. After this, you will need to strain the mixture to separate the oil from the solids. Finally, you will need to distill the oil to remove any remaining impurities and to get the final essential oil. This can be done using a steam distiller or a simple distillation apparatus. Once the oil is distilled, it can be bottled and stored in a cool, dark place.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal essential oils for upset stomach

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for upset stomach because it helps to calm down the digestive system and reduce inflammation, making it easier to digest food.
Flavonoids are good for upset stomach because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help calm down the digestive system and reduce irritation in the stomach and intestines.

Polyphenols are good for an upset stomach because they help to reduce inflammation in the digestive system and prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause stomach problems, such as nausea and diarrhea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil is obtained from the roots of the licorice plant. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an upset stomach. The oil can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can be a major cause of stomach discomfort. It also has antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and prevent cramps. This can be especially helpful for people who experience stomach cramps or diarrhea due to irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). The oil can also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to stomach problems. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria in the digestive tract, reducing the risk of infections. It may also help stimulate digestion and relieve nausea.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Carum carvi herbal essential oil, also known as caraway oil, which helps with upset stomach, start by harvesting the seeds of the Carum carvi plant, also known as caraway or meridian fennel. The seeds should be dried in a well-ventilated area, away from direct sunlight, to prevent them from getting spoiled. Next, use a carrier oil like sweet almond oil or grapeseed oil to extract the essential oil from the seeds. You can do this by placing the dried seeds in a clean glass jar, then adding the carrier oil to cover the seeds completely. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the seeds release their essential oil into the carrier oil. After the extraction period, strain the oil using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container. Discard the seeds and keep the extracted oil in a dark glass bottle. You can use this caraway oil in aromatherapy to help soothe an upset stomach, or add a few drops to a warm bath for relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for upset stomach

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Tannins are good for an upset stomach because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive tract, allowing for a faster recovery and relief from stomach discomfort.

Quinic acid is good for upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort in the stomach and intestines.
Quercetin is good for upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the digestive tract, which can calm and soothe an irritated stomach.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis essential oil comes from the bark of the Cinchona tree. This oil is good for an upset stomach because it contains compounds that help relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines. When these muscles are tense, they can cause nausea, cramps, and diarrhea. The Cinchona oil helps calm them down, which can reduce the symptoms of an upset stomach. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the stomach. This can be especially helpful if the stomach upset is caused by a bacterial or viral infection. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis essential oil has natural antispasmodic properties, which can help ease stomach cramps and spasms. This can be beneficial for people who experience stomach discomfort after eating certain foods or experiencing stress.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Carum carvi herbal essential oil, also known as caraway seed essential oil, you will need dried caraway seeds, a carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil, and a clean glass container. Start by measuring out a small amount of dried caraway seeds and placing them in a clean glass jar. Next, add a carrier oil to the jar, making sure the seeds are completely covered. The general ratio is one part seeds to three parts carrier oil. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the seeds release their oils into the carrier oil. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Your caraway seed essential oil is now ready to use, but be sure to dilute it with a carrier oil before applying it to the skin or using it in aromatherapy, as it can be quite potent.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal essential oils for upset stomach

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for upset stomach because it helps to protect and heal the lining of the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome and other digestive issues.
Kaempferol is good for upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing irritation and discomfort in the stomach and intestines.
Isorhapontigenin is good for upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the stomach, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by digestive issues.
Bilobalide is good for an upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive system and reduce irritation and inflammation in the stomach lining.
Ginkgolide B is good for upset stomach because it helps to relax the muscles in the digestive system, reducing inflammation and easing pain and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oil may help calm an upset stomach because it has natural soothing properties. This oil contains compounds that can help reduce inflammation and ease digestive discomfort. When taken internally, Ginkgo biloba oil can help relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, allowing for better digestion and less nausea. Additionally, it may also help to regulate the body's natural gut bacteria, which can become imbalanced and contribute to stomach issues. Furthermore, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce irritation and discomfort in the stomach lining, making it a potentially helpful remedy for stomach upset.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Carum carvi herbal essential oil that helps with an upset stomach, you will need fresh caraway seeds and a solvent like jojoba oil or sweet almond oil. First, dry the fresh caraway seeds in a warm place or using a food dehydrator on a low temperature setting until they are completely dry. Then, grind the seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, fill a clean glass jar with the ground seeds and pour the solvent over them, making sure that the seeds are completely covered. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This allows the oil to infuse into the solvent and extract the beneficial properties of the caraway seeds. After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Carum carvi herbal essential oil is now ready to use. It can be applied topically to the abdomen to help soothe an upset stomach, or added to a carrier oil for massage.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils for upset stomach

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Carvone is good for upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm digestive issues and reduce nausea and vomiting.
Limonene is good for upset stomach because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive tract and reduce nausea and discomfort.

Pinene is good for an upset stomach because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing nausea, cramps, and other symptoms associated with an upset stomach.
Tannins are good for upset stomach because they help soothe and calm the digestive system by reducing inflammation and preventing bacteria from growing, thereby easing stomach discomfort and pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Carum carvi, also known as caraway essential oil, has been used for centuries to help soothe an upset stomach. The main reason for this is that it has natural properties that can help calm the digestive system. Carum carvi oil contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce swelling and irritation in the stomach and intestines. This can help alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and nausea. Additionally, the oil has been known to help stimulate digestion and relieve cramps, making it a popular remedy for stomach discomfort. Its antibacterial properties can also help eliminate any underlying infections that may be contributing to the upset stomach.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Carum carvi herbal essential oil, also known as caraway oil, which helps with an upset stomach, you will need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. Start by collecting a few tablespoons of dried caraway seeds, which can be obtained from a health food store or grown in your garden. Next, place the seeds in a clean glass jar and add a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil. The ratio of seeds to oil is usually around 1:5, so for every tablespoon of seeds, use five tablespoons of oil. Now, store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the seeds infuse their goodness into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the seeds. The resulting oil can be used in aromatherapy to help soothe an upset stomach, or it can be added to a warm bath for relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Carum carvi herbal essential oils for upset stomach

intro_desc: When you have an upset stomach, it can be really uncomfortable and affect your daily life. Herbal essential oils like Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, can help. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive system and reduce nausea. It's often used to treat stomach cramps, diarrhea, and vomiting.  Another herb that can help is Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint oil. Peppermint oil can ease digestive issues like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and indigestion by relaxing the muscles in the stomach and improving digestion. It can also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can sometimes trigger stomach problems.  It's essential to address upset stomach issues because they can lead to more severe problems like dehydration, electrolyte imbalances, and malnutrition if left untreated. Moreover, constant stomach discomfort can cause stress, fatigue, and decreased productivity, making it challenging to perform daily tasks and enjoy life. By incorporating ginger and peppermint essential oils into your routine, you can alleviate stomach discomfort, improve your digestion, and enhance your overall well-being. Regularly using these oils can also help you feel more energized, focused, and motivated, allowing you to tackle your daily responsibilities with ease and confidence.
supplementary_best: To remedy an upset stomach, combining Foeniculum vulgare (fennel oil) and Glycyrrhiza glabra (licorice root oil) is a good option. Fennel oil has natural carminative properties, which help to release trapped gas in the digestive system, providing fast relief from bloating and discomfort. Licorice root oil, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, helping to calm stomach cramps and spasms. Additionally, the sweetness of licorice root oil can help to balance out the earthy flavor of fennel oil, creating a more pleasant and effective combination.  However, to enhance the effects of this combination and address the root cause of stomach upset, adding a small amount of Zingiber officinale (ginger oil) is beneficial. Ginger oil has natural digestive enzymes that help to stimulate digestion and relieve nausea. Its spicy warmth also helps to increase circulation and reduce inflammation, further alleviating stomach discomfort.  Combining these three oils in a 40% fennel oil, 30% licorice root oil, and 30% ginger oil blend provides a well-rounded remedy for upset stomach. The anti-inflammatory properties of licorice root oil and ginger oil work together to soothe the stomach, while the carminative properties of fennel oil help to release trapped gas and provide fast relief from discomfort.
supplementary_avoid: If you have an upset stomach, it's best to avoid certain herbal essential oils that can irritate or worsen digestive issues. One such oil is Peppermint's cousin, 'Eucalyptus globulus', which can cause stomach cramps and diarrhea due to its intense, cooling properties. Another oil to be cautious with is 'Lavandula angustifolia', commonly known as Lavender, although generally considered safe, its potency can sometimes trigger stomach issues in sensitive individuals. 'Cistus ladanum' or Rock Rose oil should also be used with care as it may cause nausea and stomach discomfort due to its strong, sedative properties. Additionally, 'Cassia alba' or Chinese Cinnamon oil can be too intense for sensitive stomachs, potentially leading to stomach pain and nausea. Lastly, 'Juniperus communis' or Juniper berry oil, should be used with caution as it can stimulate digestion, which may exacerbate stomach issues for some people.
supplementary_other_remedies: Ginger is a well-known natural remedy that can help with upset stomach. It contains compounds that help reduce inflammation and ease digestion. When you eat ginger, it goes into your stomach and helps calm the muscles, preventing cramps and spasms. This can be especially helpful for people who experience nausea or vomiting.  Another natural remedy is probiotics, which are found in fermented foods like yogurt or kefir. Probiotics are good bacteria that live in your gut and help keep your digestive system healthy. If the balance of good and bad bacteria gets out of whack, it can lead to stomach problems. Probiotics can help restore the balance and reduce symptoms like bloating and gas.  Apple cider vinegar has also been used to soothe an upset stomach. It contains acetic acid, which can help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause stomach problems. Drinking apple cider vinegar mixed with water can help ease digestion and reduce symptoms like heartburn and acid reflux.  Some people also use activated charcoal to help with stomach problems. Activated charcoal works by absorbing toxins and gas in the gut, which can help reduce symptoms like bloating and discomfort. When you take activated charcoal, it can also help reduce nausea and vomiting.  Aloe vera juice is another natural remedy that can help with an upset stomach. It contains compounds that can help soothe the lining of the stomach and reduce inflammation. Drinking aloe vera juice can help ease digestion and reduce symptoms like heartburn and acid reflux.  These natural remedies can be especially helpful for people who experience stomach problems on a regular basis. By trying these remedies, you can find what works best for you and reduce your symptoms.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have an upset stomach may also experience nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal cramps. These symptoms are often caused by inflammation or irritation in the digestive tract. Certain medicinal herbs can help alleviate these symptoms. Ginger, for example, has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive tract and reduce nausea. Peppermint oil, on the other hand, can relax the muscles in the stomach and help ease cramps. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, which can help with stomach ulcers and heartburn. Dandelion root can help stimulate digestion and reduce water retention, which can help with bloating and gas. Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which has strong anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). These herbs work by reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and stimulating digestion, which can help heal the digestive system and alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach.
